attributes.action: Our Street Left Behind - Full Fibre Broadband for the street removed from plans.
attributes.additional_details: Beechwood Avenue in Rutherglen was originally included in Openreach plans for full fibre broadband. However, due to issues accessing private land, our street was removed from the rollout. Nearby streets already have ultrafast fibre while our community has been left behind with outdated slower broadband. In 2026, reliable internet is essential for education, work, healthcare and communication. We believe our street deserves equal access to modern broadband infrastructure.
attributes.background: Residents of Beechwood Avenue, Rutherglen were removed from Openreach full fibre plans due to private land access issues. Nearby streets already have ultrafast fibre while our community has been left behind with outdated slower broadband. We call for equal broadband access for our street.

rejection.details:
matters relating to broadband provision are devolved, so this is a matter for the Scottish Government not the UK Government - you may wish to go to the Scottish Parliament petition site and start a petition there: 

https://petitions.parliament.scot/
